commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Sing unto the LORD, O you saints of his, and give thanks at the remembrance of his holiness. Augustine of Hippo 5. "Sing to the Lord, O ye saints of His. " The prophet seeing these future things, rejoiceth, and saith, "Sing to the Lord, O ye saints of His. And make confession of the remembrance of His holiness" (ver. And make confession to Him, that He hath not forgotten the sanctification, wherewith He hath sanctified you, although all this intermediate period belong to your desires.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Saints. Hebrew, "who have obtained mercy. "Priests and faithful people come to return thanks, because God has turned away the scourge. (Calmet)
Memory, or name, Exodus iii. 15. (Calmet)
It is from God, and not from ourselves, that holiness comes. (Worthington)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
